meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Spirit Airlines begins ‘wind-down’, cancels all flights over fuel crisis
items.summary: The collapse of the US-based budget carrier due to a doubling in jet fuel prices will cost thousands of jobs.

items.title: UK’s Starmer eyes banning some pro-Palestine protests
items.summary: PM Keir Starmer says the phrase 'globalise the Intifada' should be 'completely off limits'.

items.title: Who Is Nayib Bukele? El Salvador’s ‘coolest dictator’
items.summary: At 44, Bukele has built the world’s highest imprisonment rate and scrapped presidential term limits. What comes next?

items.title: China’s UN Envoy: Hormuz closure will dominate Trump-Xi talks
items.summary: China's UN Ambassador Fu Cong says maintaining the ceasefire and reopening the Strait of Hormuz are “urgent” priorities.

items.title: Nepal celebrates return of stolen 13th-century Buddha statue from New York
items.summary: The Himalayan nation restores centuries-old statue, stolen in the 1980s, to its original temple in capital Kathmandu.

items.title: Man Utd vs Liverpool: Premier League – preview, team news, start, lineups
items.summary: Man United just need two points to secure qualification, while Liverpool can draw level with the Red Devils with a win.

items.title: China urges reversal of UNIFIL departure from Lebanon as conflict escalates
items.summary: The UNIFIL mission has faced a growing number of casualties as Israel and Hezbollah continue to fight.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 64 as Trump rejects Tehran’s proposal
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says the latest Iranian peace proposal includes demands he 'can’t agree to'.

items.title: Pistons and Raptors survive while LA Lakers progress in NBA playoffs
items.summary: Pistons mount astounding comeback against Magic, Lakers finally see off Houston, Raptors set up decider against Cavs.

items.title: US embassy warns citizens in UK as threat level raised to ‘severe’
items.summary: US embassy in UK has advised citizens to avoids schools, churches, tourist locations and vary their travel routes.

items.title: Trump says US forces are ‘like pirates’ taking Iranian oil
items.summary: Donald Trump says US forces are ‘like pirates’ taking over Iranian ships and cargo near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Iran war protester speaks to Al Jazeera from top of Washington bridge
items.summary: A 45-year old man is occupying the top of Washington’s Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge to protest the war on Iran.

items.title: Havana slams new Trump sanctions as ‘collective punishment’ of Cuban people
items.summary: The Cuban government has firmly rejected the recent sanctions levied by US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Peru probes trafficking of citizens to fight for Russia in Ukraine
items.summary: Peru's public prosecutor says many of their citizens are victims of deception, lured by ⁠the promise of jobs.

items.title: K-pop’s BTS comeback tour rallies South Korea’s global ‘soft power’ drive
items.summary: K-pop sensations BTS ignited a wave of interest in South Korean culture that now extends from food to cosmetics.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump says no ‘early’ end to war, unhappy with Tehran offer
items.summary: White House tells Congress that Iran hostilities have 'terminated' despite continued US troop presence in Middle East.

items.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
items.summary: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

items.title: Trump considering option to ‘blast the hell out of’ Iran
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said he is "not happy" with the latest peace proposal from Iran.

items.title: Trump expands red snapper fishing as critics warn of overfishing
items.summary: Anglers in Florida, Georgia, and Carolinas gain more fishing control with Trump's red snapper permit approval.

items.title: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon
items.summary: Israel's strikes continue, with more than 2,600 killed since March 2, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

items.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised as health deteriorates
items.summary: Mohammadi has lost consciousness twice and suffered a severe cardiac crisis, her foundation has announced.

items.title: May Day rallies sweep US, demanding reforms for working-class rights
items.summary: May Day events include calls to 'tax the rich' and abolish ICE amid $70bn proposed funding for immigration enforcement.

items.title: Palestinian football boss refuses Israeli handshake at FIFA event
items.summary: Palestine’s FA President, Jibril Rajoub, explained his refusal to shake hands with Israeli FA representative.

items.title: Cuban man is 18th person to die in US ICE custody this year: Monitor
items.summary: Rights group says believed suicide underscores need for more oversight amid Trump's mass deportation drive.

items.title: Barcelona in sight of La Liga title a week before El Clasico with Real
items.summary: Barcelona face Osasuna in La Liga on Saturday where a win will heap more pressure on Real Madrid at Espanyol on Sunday.
